2282	}
2283
2284	foreach(lc, indexlist)
2285	{
2286		bool		keep = true;
2287		ListCell   *k;
2288
2289		index = lfirst(lc);
2290
2291		/* if it's pkey, it's already in finalindexlist */
2292		if (index == cxt->pkey)
2293			continue;
2294
2295		foreach(k, finalindexlist)
2296		{
2297			IndexStmt  *priorindex = lfirst(k);
2298
2299			if (equal(index->indexParams, priorindex->indexParams) &&
2300				equal(index->indexIncludingParams, priorindex->indexIncludingParams) &&
2301				equal(index->whereClause, priorindex->whereClause) &&
2302				equal(index->excludeOpNames, priorindex->excludeOpNames) &&
2303				strcmp(index->accessMethod, priorindex->accessMethod) == 0 &&
2304				index->nulls_not_distinct == priorindex->nulls_not_distinct &&
2305				index->deferrable == priorindex->deferrable &&
2306				index->initdeferred == priorindex->initdeferred)
2307			{
2308				priorindex->unique |= index->unique;
2309
2310				/*
2311				 * If the prior index is as yet unnamed, and this one is
2312				 * named, then transfer the name to the prior index. This
2313				 * ensures that if we have named and unnamed constraints,
2314				 * we'll use (at least one of) the names for the index.
2315				 */
2316				if (priorindex->idxname == NULL)
2317					priorindex->idxname = index->idxname;
2318				keep = false;
2319				break;
2320			}
2321		}
2322
2323		if (keep)
2324			finalindexlist = lappend(finalindexlist, index);
2325	}
2326
2327	/*
2328	 * Now append all the IndexStmts to cxt->alist.
2329	 */
2330	cxt->alist = list_concat(cxt->alist, finalindexlist);
2331}
2332
2333/*
2334 * transformIndexConstraint
2335 *		Transform one UNIQUE, PRIMARY KEY, or EXCLUDE constraint for
2336 *		transformIndexConstraints. An IndexStmt is returned.
2337 *
2338 * For a PRIMARY KEY constraint, we additionally create not-null constraints
2339 * for columns that don't already have them.
2340 */
2341static IndexStmt *
2342transformIndexConstraint(Constraint *constraint, CreateStmtContext *cxt)
2343{
2344	IndexStmt  *index;
2345	ListCell   *lc;
2346
2347	index = makeNode(IndexStmt);
2348
2349	index->unique = (constraint->contype != CONSTR_EXCLUSION);
2350	index->primary = (constraint->contype == CONSTR_PRIMARY);
2351	if (index->primary)
2352	{
2353		if (cxt->pkey != NULL)
2354			ereport(ERROR,
2355					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_TABLE_DEFINITION),
2356					 errmsg("multiple primary keys for table \"%s\" are not allowed",
2357							cxt->relation->relname),
2358					 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, constraint->location)));
2359		cxt->pkey = index;
2360
2361		/*
2362		 * In ALTER TABLE case, a primary index might already exist, but
2363		 * DefineIndex will check for it.
2364		 */
2365	}
2366	index->nulls_not_distinct = constraint->nulls_not_distinct;
2367	index->isconstraint = true;
2368	index->iswithoutoverlaps = constraint->without_overlaps;
2369	index->deferrable = constraint->deferrable;
2370	index->initdeferred = constraint->initdeferred;
2371
2372	if (constraint->conname != NULL)
2373		index->idxname = pstrdup(constraint->conname);
2374	else
2375		index->idxname = NULL;	/* DefineIndex will choose name */
2376
2377	index->relation = cxt->relation;
2378	index->accessMethod = constraint->access_method ? constraint->access_method : DEFAULT_INDEX_TYPE;
2379	index->options = constraint->options;
2380	index->tableSpace = constraint->indexspace;
2381	index->whereClause = constraint->where_clause;
2382	index->indexParams = NIL;
2383	index->indexIncludingParams = NIL;
2384	index->excludeOpNames = NIL;
2385	index->idxcomment = NULL;
2386	index->indexOid = InvalidOid;
2387	index->oldNumber = InvalidRelFileNumber;
2388	index->oldCreateSubid = InvalidSubTransactionId;
2389	index->oldFirstRelfilelocatorSubid = InvalidSubTransactionId;
2390	index->transformed = false;
2391	index->concurrent = false;
2392	index->if_not_exists = false;
2393	index->reset_default_tblspc = constraint->reset_default_tblspc;
2394
2395	/*
2396	 * If it's ALTER TABLE ADD CONSTRAINT USING INDEX, look up the index and
2397	 * verify it's usable, then extract the implied column name list.  (We
2398	 * will not actually need the column name list at runtime, but we need it
2399	 * now to check for duplicate column entries below.)
2400	 */
2401	if (constraint->indexname != NULL)
2402	{
2403		char	   *index_name = constraint->indexname;
2404		Relation	heap_rel = cxt->rel;
2405		Oid			index_oid;
2406		Relation	index_rel;
2407		Form_pg_index index_form;
2408		oidvector  *indclass;
2409		Datum		indclassDatum;
2410		int			i;
2411
2412		/* Grammar should not allow this with explicit column list */
2413		Assert(constraint->keys == NIL);
2414
2415		/* Grammar should only allow PRIMARY and UNIQUE constraints */
2416		Assert(constraint->contype == CONSTR_PRIMARY ||
2417			   constraint->contype == CONSTR_UNIQUE);
2418
2419		/* Must be ALTER, not CREATE, but grammar doesn't enforce that */
2420		if (!cxt->isalter)
2421			ereport(ERROR,
2422					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
2423					 errmsg("cannot use an existing index in CREATE TABLE"),
2424					 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, constraint->location)));
2425
2426		/* Look for the index in the same schema as the table */
2427		index_oid = get_relname_relid(index_name, RelationGetNamespace(heap_rel));
2428
2429		if (!OidIsValid(index_oid))
2430			ereport(ERROR,
2431					(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
2432					 errmsg("index \"%s\" does not exist", index_name),
2433					 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, constraint->location)));
2434
2435		/* Open the index (this will throw an error if it is not an index) */
2436		index_rel = index_open(index_oid, AccessShareLock);
2437		index_form = index_rel->rd_index;
2438
2439		/* Check that it does not have an associated constraint already */
2440		if (OidIsValid(get_index_constraint(index_oid)))
2441			ereport(ERROR,
2442					(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
2443					 errmsg("index \"%s\" is already associated with a constraint",
2444							index_name),
2445					 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, constraint->location)));
2446
2447		/* Perform validity checks on the index */
2448		if (index_form->indrelid != RelationGetRelid(heap_rel))
2449			ereport(ERROR,
2450					(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
2451					 errmsg("index \"%s\" does not belong to table \"%s\"",
2452							index_name, RelationGetRelationName(heap_rel)),
2453					 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, constraint->location)));
2454
2455		if (!index_form->indisvalid)
2456			ereport(ERROR,
2457					(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
2458					 errmsg("index \"%s\" is not valid", index_name),
2459					 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, constraint->location)));
2460
2461		/*
2462		 * Today we forbid non-unique indexes, but we could permit GiST
2463		 * indexes whose last entry is a range type and use that to create a
2464		 * WITHOUT OVERLAPS constraint (i.e. a temporal constraint).
2465		 */
2466		if (!index_form->indisunique)
2467			ereport(ERROR,
2468					(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
2469					 errmsg("\"%s\" is not a unique index", index_name),
2470					 errdetail("Cannot create a primary key or unique constraint using such an index."),
2471					 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, constraint->location)));
2472
2473		if (RelationGetIndexExpressions(index_rel) != NIL)
2474			ereport(ERROR,
2475					(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
2476					 errmsg("index \"%s\" contains expressions", index_name),
2477					 errdetail("Cannot create a primary key or unique constraint using such an index."),
2478					 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, constraint->location)));
2479
2480		if (RelationGetIndexPredicate(index_rel) != NIL)
2481			ereport(ERROR,
2482					(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
2483					 errmsg("\"%s\" is a partial index", index_name),
2484					 errdetail("Cannot create a primary key or unique constraint using such an index."),
2485					 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, constraint->location)));
2486
2487		/*
2488		 * It's probably unsafe to change a deferred index to non-deferred. (A
2489		 * non-constraint index couldn't be deferred anyway, so this case
2490		 * should never occur; no need to sweat, but let's check it.)
2491		 */
2492		if (!index_form->indimmediate && !constraint->deferrable)
2493			ereport(ERROR,
2494					(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
2495					 errmsg("\"%s\" is a deferrable index", index_name),
2496					 errdetail("Cannot create a non-deferrable constraint using a deferrable index."),
2497					 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, constraint->location)));
2498
2499		/*
2500		 * Insist on it being a btree.  We must have an index that exactly
2501		 * matches what you'd get from plain ADD CONSTRAINT syntax, else dump
2502		 * and reload will produce a different index (breaking pg_upgrade in
2503		 * particular).
2504		 */
2505		if (index_rel->rd_rel->relam != get_index_am_oid(DEFAULT_INDEX_TYPE, false))
2506			ereport(ERROR,
2507					(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
2508					 errmsg("index \"%s\" is not a btree", index_name),
2509					 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, constraint->location)));
2510
2511		/* Must get indclass the hard way */
2512		indclassDatum = SysCacheGetAttrNotNull(INDEXRELID,
2513											   index_rel->rd_indextuple,
2514											   Anum_pg_index_indclass);
2515		indclass = (oidvector *) DatumGetPointer(indclassDatum);
2516
2517		for (i = 0; i < index_form->indnatts; i++)
2518		{
2519			int16		attnum = index_form->indkey.values[i];
2520			const FormData_pg_attribute *attform;
2521			char	   *attname;
2522			Oid			defopclass;
2523
2524			/*
2525			 * We shouldn't see attnum == 0 here, since we already rejected
2526			 * expression indexes.  If we do, SystemAttributeDefinition will
2527			 * throw an error.
2528			 */
2529			if (attnum > 0)
2530			{
2531				Assert(attnum <= heap_rel->rd_att->natts);
2532				attform = TupleDescAttr(heap_rel->rd_att, attnum - 1);
2533			}
2534			else
2535				attform = SystemAttributeDefinition(attnum);
2536			attname = pstrdup(NameStr(attform->attname));
2537
2538			if (i < index_form->indnkeyatts)
2539			{
2540				/*
2541				 * Insist on default opclass, collation, and sort options.
2542				 * While the index would still work as a constraint with
2543				 * non-default settings, it might not provide exactly the same
2544				 * uniqueness semantics as you'd get from a normally-created
2545				 * constraint; and there's also the dump/reload problem
2546				 * mentioned above.
2547				 */
2548				Datum		attoptions =
2549					get_attoptions(RelationGetRelid(index_rel), i + 1);
2550
2551				defopclass = GetDefaultOpClass(attform->atttypid,
2552											   index_rel->rd_rel->relam);
2553				if (indclass->values[i] != defopclass ||
2554					attform->attcollation != index_rel->rd_indcollation[i] ||
2555					attoptions != (Datum) 0 ||
2556					index_rel->rd_indoption[i] != 0)
2557					ereport(ERROR,
2558							(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
2559							 errmsg("index \"%s\" column number %d does not have default sorting behavior", index_name, i + 1),
2560							 errdetail("Cannot create a primary key or unique constraint using such an index."),
2561							 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, constraint->location)));
2562
2563				/* If a PK, ensure the columns get not null constraints */
2564				if (constraint->contype == CONSTR_PRIMARY)
2565					cxt->nnconstraints =
2566						lappend(cxt->nnconstraints,
2567								makeNotNullConstraint(makeString(attname)));
2568
2569				constraint->keys = lappend(constraint->keys, makeString(attname));
2570			}
2571			else
2572				constraint->including = lappend(constraint->including, makeString(attname));
2573		}
2574
2575		/* Close the index relation but keep the lock */
2576		relation_close(index_rel, NoLock);
2577
2578		index->indexOid = index_oid;
2579	}
2580
2581	/*
2582	 * If it's an EXCLUDE constraint, the grammar returns a list of pairs of
2583	 * IndexElems and operator names.  We have to break that apart into
2584	 * separate lists.
2585	 */
2586	if (constraint->contype == CONSTR_EXCLUSION)
2587	{
2588		foreach(lc, constraint->exclusions)
2589		{
2590			List	   *pair = (List *) lfirst(lc);
2591			IndexElem  *elem;
2592			List	   *opname;
2593
2594			Assert(list_length(pair) == 2);
2595			elem = linitial_node(IndexElem, pair);
2596			opname = lsecond_node(List, pair);
2597
2598			index->indexParams = lappend(index->indexParams, elem);
2599			index->excludeOpNames = lappend(index->excludeOpNames, opname);
2600		}
2601	}
2602
2603	/*
2604	 * For UNIQUE and PRIMARY KEY, we just have a list of column names.
2605	 *
2606	 * Make sure referenced keys exist.  If we are making a PRIMARY KEY index,
2607	 * also make sure they are not-null.  For WITHOUT OVERLAPS constraints, we
2608	 * make sure the last part is a range or multirange.
2609	 */
2610	else
2611	{
2612		foreach(lc, constraint->keys)
2613		{
2614			char	   *key = strVal(lfirst(lc));
2615			bool		found = false;
2616			ColumnDef  *column = NULL;
2617			ListCell   *columns;
2618			IndexElem  *iparam;
2619			Oid			typid = InvalidOid;
2620
2621			/* Make sure referenced column exists. */
2622			foreach(columns, cxt->columns)
2623			{
2624				column = lfirst_node(ColumnDef, columns);
2625				if (strcmp(column->colname, key) == 0)
2626				{
2627					found = true;
2628					break;
2629				}
2630			}
2631			if (!found)
2632				column = NULL;
2633
2634			if (found)
2635			{
2636				/*
2637				 * column is defined in the new table.  For CREATE TABLE with
2638				 * a PRIMARY KEY, we can apply the not-null constraint cheaply
2639				 * here.  If the not-null constraint already exists, we can
2640				 * (albeit not so cheaply) verify that it's not a NO INHERIT
2641				 * constraint.
2642				 *
2643				 * Note that ALTER TABLE never needs either check, because
2644				 * those constraints have already been added by
2645				 * ATPrepAddPrimaryKey.
2646				 */
2647				if (constraint->contype == CONSTR_PRIMARY &&
2648					!cxt->isalter)
2649				{
2650					if (column->is_not_null)
2651					{
2652						foreach_node(Constraint, nn, cxt->nnconstraints)
2653						{
2654							if (strcmp(strVal(linitial(nn->keys)), key) == 0)
2655							{
2656								if (nn->is_no_inherit)
2657									ereport(ERROR,
2658											errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
2659											errmsg("conflicting NO INHERIT declaration for not-null constraint on column \"%s\"",
2660												   key));
2661								break;
2662							}
2663						}
2664					}
2665					else
2666					{
2667						column->is_not_null = true;
2668						cxt->nnconstraints =
2669							lappend(cxt->nnconstraints,
2670									makeNotNullConstraint(makeString(key)));
2671					}
2672				}
2673				else if (constraint->contype == CONSTR_PRIMARY)
2674					Assert(column->is_not_null);
2675			}
2676			else if (SystemAttributeByName(key) != NULL)
2677			{
2678				/*
2679				 * column will be a system column in the new table, so accept
2680				 * it. System columns can't ever be null, so no need to worry
2681				 * about PRIMARY/NOT NULL constraint.
2682				 */
2683				found = true;
2684			}
2685			else if (cxt->inhRelations)
2686			{
2687				/* try inherited tables */
2688				ListCell   *inher;
2689
2690				foreach(inher, cxt->inhRelations)
2691				{
2692					RangeVar   *inh = lfirst_node(RangeVar, inher);
2693					Relation	rel;
2694					int			count;
2695
2696					rel = table_openrv(inh, AccessShareLock);
2697					/* check user requested inheritance from valid relkind */
2698					if (rel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_RELATION &&
2699						rel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_FOREIGN_TABLE &&
2700						rel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
2701						ereport(ERROR,
2702								(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
2703								 errmsg("inherited relation \"%s\" is not a table or foreign table",
2704										inh->relname)));
2705					for (count = 0; count < rel->rd_att->natts; count++)
2706					{
2707						Form_pg_attribute inhattr = TupleDescAttr(rel->rd_att,
2708																  count);
2709						char	   *inhname = NameStr(inhattr->attname);
2710
2711						if (inhattr->attisdropped)
2712							continue;
2713						if (strcmp(key, inhname) == 0)
2714						{
2715							found = true;
2716							typid = inhattr->atttypid;
2717
2718							if (constraint->contype == CONSTR_PRIMARY)
2719								cxt->nnconstraints =
2720									lappend(cxt->nnconstraints,
2721											makeNotNullConstraint(makeString(pstrdup(inhname))));
2722							break;
2723						}
2724					}
2725					table_close(rel, NoLock);
2726					if (found)
2727						break;
2728				}
2729			}
2730
2731			/*
2732			 * In the ALTER TABLE case, don't complain about index keys not
2733			 * created in the command; they may well exist already.
2734			 * DefineIndex will complain about them if not.
2735			 */
2736			if (!found && !cxt->isalter)
2737				ereport(ERROR,
2738						(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_COLUMN),
2739						 errmsg("column \"%s\" named in key does not exist", key),
2740						 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, constraint->location)));
2741
2742			/* Check for PRIMARY KEY(foo, foo) */
2743			foreach(columns, index->indexParams)
2744			{
2745				iparam = (IndexElem *) lfirst(columns);
2746				if (iparam->name && strcmp(key, iparam->name) == 0)
2747				{
2748					if (index->primary)
2749						ereport(ERROR,
2750								(errcode(ERRCODE_DUPLICATE_COLUMN),
2751								 errmsg("column \"%s\" appears twice in primary key constraint",
2752										key),
2753								 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, constraint->location)));
2754					else
2755						ereport(ERROR,
2756								(errcode(ERRCODE_DUPLICATE_COLUMN),
2757								 errmsg("column \"%s\" appears twice in unique constraint",
2758										key),
2759								 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, constraint->location)));
2760				}
2761			}
2762
2763			/*
2764			 * The WITHOUT OVERLAPS part (if any) must be a range or
2765			 * multirange type, or a domain over such a type.
2766			 */
2767			if (constraint->without_overlaps && lc == list_last_cell(constraint->keys))
2768			{
2769				if (!found && cxt->isalter)
2770				{
2771					/*
2772					 * Look up the column type on existing table. If we can't
2773					 * find it, let things fail in DefineIndex.
2774					 */
2775					Relation	rel = cxt->rel;
2776
2777					for (int i = 0; i < rel->rd_att->natts; i++)
2778					{
2779						Form_pg_attribute attr = TupleDescAttr(rel->rd_att, i);
2780						const char *attname;
2781
2782						if (attr->attisdropped)
2783							continue;
2784						attname = NameStr(attr->attname);
2785						if (strcmp(attname, key) == 0)
2786						{
2787							found = true;
2788							typid = attr->atttypid;
2789							break;
2790						}
2791					}
2792				}
2793				if (found)
2794				{
2795					/* Look up column type if we didn't already */
2796					if (!OidIsValid(typid) && column)
2797						typid = typenameTypeId(cxt->pstate,
2798											   column->typeName);
2799					/* Look through any domain */
2800					if (OidIsValid(typid))
2801						typid = getBaseType(typid);
2802					/* Complain if not range/multirange */
2803					if (!OidIsValid(typid) ||
2804						!(type_is_range(typid) || type_is_multirange(typid)))
2805						ereport(ERROR,
2806								(errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
2807								 errmsg("column \"%s\" in WITHOUT OVERLAPS is not a range or multirange type", key),
2808								 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, constraint->location)));
2809				}
2810			}
2811
2812			/* OK, add it to the index definition */
2813			iparam = makeNode(IndexElem);
2814			iparam->name = pstrdup(key);
2815			iparam->expr = NULL;
2816			iparam->indexcolname = NULL;
2817			iparam->collation = NIL;
2818			iparam->opclass = NIL;
2819			iparam->opclassopts = NIL;
2820			iparam->ordering = SORTBY_DEFAULT;
2821			iparam->nulls_ordering = SORTBY_NULLS_DEFAULT;
2822			index->indexParams = lappend(index->indexParams, iparam);
2823		}
2824
2825		if (constraint->without_overlaps)
2826		{
2827			/*
2828			 * This enforces that there is at least one equality column
2829			 * besides the WITHOUT OVERLAPS columns.  This is per SQL
2830			 * standard.  XXX Do we need this?
2831			 */
2832			if (list_length(constraint->keys) < 2)
2833				ereport(ERROR,
2834						errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
2835						errmsg("constraint using WITHOUT OVERLAPS needs at least two columns"));
2836
2837			/* WITHOUT OVERLAPS requires a GiST index */
2838			index->accessMethod = "gist";
2839		}
2840
2841	}
2842
2843	/*
2844	 * Add included columns to index definition.  This is much like the
2845	 * simple-column-name-list code above, except that we don't worry about
2846	 * NOT NULL marking; included columns in a primary key should not be
2847	 * forced NOT NULL.  We don't complain about duplicate columns, either,
2848	 * though maybe we should?
2849	 */
2850	foreach(lc, constraint->including)
2851	{
2852		char	   *key = strVal(lfirst(lc));
2853		bool		found = false;
2854		ColumnDef  *column = NULL;
2855		ListCell   *columns;
2856		IndexElem  *iparam;
2857
2858		foreach(columns, cxt->columns)
2859		{
2860			column = lfirst_node(ColumnDef, columns);
2861			if (strcmp(column->colname, key) == 0)
2862			{
2863				found = true;
2864				break;
2865			}
2866		}
2867
2868		if (!found)
2869		{
2870			if (SystemAttributeByName(key) != NULL)
2871			{
2872				/*
2873				 * column will be a system column in the new table, so accept
2874				 * it.
2875				 */
2876				found = true;
2877			}
2878			else if (cxt->inhRelations)
2879			{
2880				/* try inherited tables */
2881				ListCell   *inher;
2882
2883				foreach(inher, cxt->inhRelations)
2884				{
2885					RangeVar   *inh = lfirst_node(RangeVar, inher);
2886					Relation	rel;
2887					int			count;
2888
2889					rel = table_openrv(inh, AccessShareLock);
2890					/* check user requested inheritance from valid relkind */
2891					if (rel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_RELATION &&
2892						rel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_FOREIGN_TABLE &&
2893						rel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
2894						ereport(ERROR,
2895								(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
2896								 errmsg("inherited relation \"%s\" is not a table or foreign table",
2897										inh->relname)));
2898					for (count = 0; count < rel->rd_att->natts; count++)
2899					{
2900						Form_pg_attribute inhattr = TupleDescAttr(rel->rd_att,
2901																  count);
2902						char	   *inhname = NameStr(inhattr->attname);
2903
2904						if (inhattr->attisdropped)
2905							continue;
2906						if (strcmp(key, inhname) == 0)
2907						{
2908							found = true;
2909							break;
2910						}
2911					}
2912					table_close(rel, NoLock);
2913					if (found)
2914						break;
2915				}
2916			}
2917		}
2918
2919		/*
2920		 * In the ALTER TABLE case, don't complain about index keys not
2921		 * created in the command; they may well exist already. DefineIndex
2922		 * will complain about them if not.
2923		 */
2924		if (!found && !cxt->isalter)
2925			ereport(ERROR,
2926					(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_COLUMN),
2927					 errmsg("column \"%s\" named in key does not exist", key),
2928					 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, constraint->location)));
2929
2930		/* OK, add it to the index definition */
2931		iparam = makeNode(IndexElem);
2932		iparam->name = pstrdup(key);
2933		iparam->expr = NULL;
2934		iparam->indexcolname = NULL;
2935		iparam->collation = NIL;
2936		iparam->opclass = NIL;
2937		iparam->opclassopts = NIL;
2938		index->indexIncludingParams = lappend(index->indexIncludingParams, iparam);
2939	}
2940
2941	return index;
2942}
2943
2944/*
2945 * transformCheckConstraints
2946 *		handle CHECK constraints
2947 *
2948 * Right now, there's nothing to do here when called from ALTER TABLE,
2949 * but the other constraint-transformation functions are called in both
2950 * the CREATE TABLE and ALTER TABLE paths, so do the same here, and just
2951 * don't do anything if we're not authorized to skip validation.
2952 */
2953static void
2954transformCheckConstraints(CreateStmtContext *cxt, bool skipValidation)
2955{
2956	ListCell   *ckclist;
2957
2958	if (cxt->ckconstraints == NIL)
2959		return;
2960
2961	/*
2962	 * When creating a new table (but not a foreign table), we can safely skip
2963	 * the validation of check constraints and mark them as valid based on the
2964	 * constraint enforcement flag, since NOT ENFORCED constraints must always
2965	 * be marked as NOT VALID. (This will override any user-supplied NOT VALID
2966	 * flag.)
2967	 */
2968	if (skipValidation)
2969	{
2970		foreach(ckclist, cxt->ckconstraints)
2971		{
2972			Constraint *constraint = (Constraint *) lfirst(ckclist);
2973
2974			constraint->skip_validation = true;
2975			constraint->initially_valid = constraint->is_enforced;
2976		}
2977	}
2978}
2979
2980/*
2981 * transformFKConstraints
2982 *		handle FOREIGN KEY constraints
2983 */
2984static void
2985transformFKConstraints(CreateStmtContext *cxt,
2986					   bool skipValidation, bool isAddConstraint)
2987{
2988	ListCell   *fkclist;
2989
2990	if (cxt->fkconstraints == NIL)
2991		return;
2992
2993	/*
2994	 * If CREATE TABLE or adding a column with NULL default, we can safely
2995	 * skip validation of FK constraints, and mark them as valid based on the
2996	 * constraint enforcement flag, since NOT ENFORCED constraints must always
2997	 * be marked as NOT VALID. (This will override any user-supplied NOT VALID
2998	 * flag.)
2999	 */
3000	if (skipValidation)
3001	{
3002		foreach(fkclist, cxt->fkconstraints)
3003		{
3004			Constraint *constraint = (Constraint *) lfirst(fkclist);
3005
3006			constraint->skip_validation = true;
3007			constraint->initially_valid = constraint->is_enforced;
3008		}
3009	}
3010
3011	/*
3012	 * For CREATE TABLE or ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN, gin up an ALTER TABLE ADD
3013	 * CONSTRAINT command to execute after the basic command is complete. (If
3014	 * called from ADD CONSTRAINT, that routine will add the FK constraints to
3015	 * its own subcommand list.)
3016	 *
3017	 * Note: the ADD CONSTRAINT command must also execute after any index
3018	 * creation commands.  Thus, this should run after
3019	 * transformIndexConstraints, so that the CREATE INDEX commands are
3020	 * already in cxt->alist.  See also the handling of cxt->likeclauses.
3021	 */
3022	if (!isAddConstraint)
3023	{
3024		AlterTableStmt *alterstmt = makeNode(AlterTableStmt);
3025
3026		alterstmt->relation = cxt->relation;
3027		alterstmt->cmds = NIL;
3028		alterstmt->objtype = OBJECT_TABLE;
3029
3030		foreach(fkclist, cxt->fkconstraints)
3031		{
3032			Constraint *constraint = (Constraint *) lfirst(fkclist);
3033			AlterTableCmd *altercmd = makeNode(AlterTableCmd);
3034
3035			altercmd->subtype = AT_AddConstraint;
3036			altercmd->name = NULL;
3037			altercmd->def = (Node *) constraint;
3038			alterstmt->cmds = lappend(alterstmt->cmds, altercmd);
3039		}
3040
3041		cxt->alist = lappend(cxt->alist, alterstmt);
3042	}
3043}
3044
3045/*
3046 * transformIndexStmt - parse analysis for CREATE INDEX and ALTER TABLE
3047 *
3048 * Note: this is a no-op for an index not using either index expressions or
3049 * a predicate expression.  There are several code paths that create indexes
3050 * without bothering to call this, because they know they don't have any
3051 * such expressions to deal with.
3052 *
3053 * To avoid race conditions, it's important that this function rely only on
3054 * the passed-in relid (and not on stmt->relation) to determine the target
3055 * relation.
3056 */
3057IndexStmt *
3058transformIndexStmt(Oid relid, IndexStmt *stmt, const char *queryString)
3059{
3060	ParseState *pstate;
3061	ParseNamespaceItem *nsitem;
3062	ListCell   *l;
3063	Relation	rel;
3064
3065	/* Nothing to do if statement already transformed. */
3066	if (stmt->transformed)
3067		return stmt;
3068
3069	/* Set up pstate */
3070	pstate = make_parsestate(NULL);
3071	pstate->p_sourcetext = queryString;
3072
3073	/*
3074	 * Put the parent table into the rtable so that the expressions can refer
3075	 * to its fields without qualification.  Caller is responsible for locking
3076	 * relation, but we still need to open it.
3077	 */
3078	rel = relation_open(relid, NoLock);
3079	nsitem = addRangeTableEntryForRelation(pstate, rel,
3080										   AccessShareLock,
3081										   NULL, false, true);
3082
3083	/* no to join list, yes to namespaces */
3084	addNSItemToQuery(pstate, nsitem, false, true, true);
3085
3086	/* take care of the where clause */
3087	if (stmt->whereClause)
3088	{
3089		stmt->whereClause = transformWhereClause(pstate,
3090												 stmt->whereClause,
3091												 EXPR_KIND_INDEX_PREDICATE,
3092												 "WHERE");
3093		/* we have to fix its collations too */
3094		assign_expr_collations(pstate, stmt->whereClause);
3095	}
3096
3097	/* take care of any index expressions */
3098	foreach(l, stmt->indexParams)
3099	{
3100		IndexElem  *ielem = (IndexElem *) lfirst(l);
3101
3102		if (ielem->expr)
3103		{
3104			/* Extract preliminary index col name before transforming expr */
3105			if (ielem->indexcolname == NULL)
3106				ielem->indexcolname = FigureIndexColname(ielem->expr);
3107
3108			/* Now do parse transformation of the expression */
3109			ielem->expr = transformExpr(pstate, ielem->expr,
3110										EXPR_KIND_INDEX_EXPRESSION);
3111
3112			/* We have to fix its collations too */
3113			assign_expr_collations(pstate, ielem->expr);
3114
3115			/*
3116			 * transformExpr() should have already rejected subqueries,
3117			 * aggregates, window functions, and SRFs, based on the EXPR_KIND_
3118			 * for an index expression.
3119			 *
3120			 * DefineIndex() will make more checks.
3121			 */
3122		}
3123	}
3124
3125	/*
3126	 * Check that only the base rel is mentioned.  (This should be dead code
3127	 * now that add_missing_from is history.)
3128	 */
3129	if (list_length(pstate->p_rtable) != 1)
3130		ereport(ERROR,
3131				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_COLUMN_REFERENCE),
3132				 errmsg("index expressions and predicates can refer only to the table being indexed")));
3133
3134	free_parsestate(pstate);
3135
3136	/* Close relation */
3137	table_close(rel, NoLock);
3138
3139	/* Mark statement as successfully transformed */
3140	stmt->transformed = true;
3141
3142	return stmt;
3143}
3144
3145/*
3146 * transformStatsStmt - parse analysis for CREATE STATISTICS
3147 *
3148 * To avoid race conditions, it's important that this function relies only on
3149 * the passed-in relid (and not on stmt->relation) to determine the target
3150 * relation.
3151 */
3152CreateStatsStmt *
3153transformStatsStmt(Oid relid, CreateStatsStmt *stmt, const char *queryString)
3154{
3155	ParseState *pstate;
3156	ParseNamespaceItem *nsitem;
3157	ListCell   *l;
3158	Relation	rel;
3159
3160	/* Nothing to do if statement already transformed. */
3161	if (stmt->transformed)
3162		return stmt;
3163
3164	/* Set up pstate */
3165	pstate = make_parsestate(NULL);
3166	pstate->p_sourcetext = queryString;
3167
3168	/*
3169	 * Put the parent table into the rtable so that the expressions can refer
3170	 * to its fields without qualification.  Caller is responsible for locking
3171	 * relation, but we still need to open it.
3172	 */
3173	rel = relation_open(relid, NoLock);
3174	nsitem = addRangeTableEntryForRelation(pstate, rel,
3175										   AccessShareLock,
3176										   NULL, false, true);
3177
3178	/* no to join list, yes to namespaces */
3179	addNSItemToQuery(pstate, nsitem, false, true, true);
3180
3181	/* take care of any expressions */
3182	foreach(l, stmt->exprs)
3183	{
3184		StatsElem  *selem = (StatsElem *) lfirst(l);
3185
3186		if (selem->expr)
3187		{
3188			/* Now do parse transformation of the expression */
3189			selem->expr = transformExpr(pstate, selem->expr,
3190										EXPR_KIND_STATS_EXPRESSION);
3191
3192			/* We have to fix its collations too */
3193			assign_expr_collations(pstate, selem->expr);
3194		}
3195	}
3196
3197	/*
3198	 * Check that only the base rel is mentioned.  (This should be dead code
3199	 * now that add_missing_from is history.)
3200	 */
3201	if (list_length(pstate->p_rtable) != 1)
3202		ereport(ERROR,
3203				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_COLUMN_REFERENCE),
3204				 errmsg("statistics expressions can refer only to the table being referenced")));
3205
3206	free_parsestate(pstate);
3207
3208	/* Close relation */
3209	table_close(rel, NoLock);
3210
3211	/* Mark statement as successfully transformed */
3212	stmt->transformed = true;
3213
3214	return stmt;
3215}
3216
3217
3218/*
3219 * transformRuleStmt -
3220 *	  transform a CREATE RULE Statement. The action is a list of parse
3221 *	  trees which is transformed into a list of query trees, and we also
3222 *	  transform the WHERE clause if any.
3223 *
3224 * actions and whereClause are output parameters that receive the
3225 * transformed results.
3226 */
3227void
3228transformRuleStmt(RuleStmt *stmt, const char *queryString,
3229				  List **actions, Node **whereClause)
3230{
3231	Relation	rel;
3232	ParseState *pstate;
3233	ParseNamespaceItem *oldnsitem;
3234	ParseNamespaceItem *newnsitem;
3235
3236	/*
3237	 * To avoid deadlock, make sure the first thing we do is grab
3238	 * AccessExclusiveLock on the target relation.  This will be needed by
3239	 * DefineQueryRewrite(), and we don't want to grab a lesser lock
3240	 * beforehand.
3241	 */
3242	rel = table_openrv(stmt->relation, AccessExclusiveLock);
3243
3244	if (rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_MATVIEW)
3245		ereport(ERROR,
3246				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3247				 errmsg("rules on materialized views are not supported")));
3248
3249	/* Set up pstate */
3250	pstate = make_parsestate(NULL);
3251	pstate->p_sourcetext = queryString;
3252
3253	/*
3254	 * NOTE: 'OLD' must always have a varno equal to 1 and 'NEW' equal to 2.
3255	 * Set up their ParseNamespaceItems in the main pstate for use in parsing
3256	 * the rule qualification.
3257	 */
3258	oldnsitem = addRangeTableEntryForRelation(pstate, rel,
3259											  AccessShareLock,
3260											  makeAlias("old", NIL),
3261											  false, false);
3262	newnsitem = addRangeTableEntryForRelation(pstate, rel,
3263											  AccessShareLock,
3264											  makeAlias("new", NIL),
3265											  false, false);
3266
3267	/*
3268	 * They must be in the namespace too for lookup purposes, but only add the
3269	 * one(s) that are relevant for the current kind of rule.  In an UPDATE
3270	 * rule, quals must refer to OLD.field or NEW.field to be unambiguous, but
3271	 * there's no need to be so picky for INSERT & DELETE.  We do not add them
3272	 * to the joinlist.
3273	 */
3274	switch (stmt->event)
3275	{
3276		case CMD_SELECT:
3277			addNSItemToQuery(pstate, oldnsitem, false, true, true);
3278			break;
3279		case CMD_UPDATE:
3280			addNSItemToQuery(pstate, oldnsitem, false, true, true);
3281			addNSItemToQuery(pstate, newnsitem, false, true, true);
3282			break;
3283		case CMD_INSERT:
3284			addNSItemToQuery(pstate, newnsitem, false, true, true);
3285			break;
3286		case CMD_DELETE:
3287			addNSItemToQuery(pstate, oldnsitem, false, true, true);
3288			break;
3289		default:
3290			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized event type: %d",
3291				 (int) stmt->event);
3292			break;
3293	}
3294
3295	/* take care of the where clause */
3296	*whereClause = transformWhereClause(pstate,
3297										stmt->whereClause,
3298										EXPR_KIND_WHERE,
3299										"WHERE");
3300	/* we have to fix its collations too */
3301	assign_expr_collations(pstate, *whereClause);
3302
3303	/* this is probably dead code without add_missing_from: */
3304	if (list_length(pstate->p_rtable) != 2) /* naughty, naughty... */
3305		ereport(ERROR,
3306				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
3307				 errmsg("rule WHERE condition cannot contain references to other relations")));
3308
3309	/*
3310	 * 'instead nothing' rules with a qualification need a query rangetable so
3311	 * the rewrite handler can add the negated rule qualification to the
3312	 * original query. We create a query with the new command type CMD_NOTHING
3313	 * here that is treated specially by the rewrite system.
3314	 */
3315	if (stmt->actions == NIL)
3316	{
3317		Query	   *nothing_qry = makeNode(Query);
3318
3319		nothing_qry->commandType = CMD_NOTHING;
3320		nothing_qry->rtable = pstate->p_rtable;
3321		nothing_qry->rteperminfos = pstate->p_rteperminfos;
3322		nothing_qry->jointree = makeFromExpr(NIL, NULL);	/* no join wanted */
3323
3324		*actions = list_make1(nothing_qry);
3325	}
3326	else
3327	{
3328		ListCell   *l;
3329		List	   *newactions = NIL;
3330
3331		/*
3332		 * transform each statement, like parse_sub_analyze()
3333		 */
3334		foreach(l, stmt->actions)
3335		{
3336			Node	   *action = (Node *) lfirst(l);
3337			ParseState *sub_pstate = make_parsestate(NULL);
3338			Query	   *sub_qry,
3339					   *top_subqry;
3340			bool		has_old,
3341						has_new;
3342
3343			/*
3344			 * Since outer ParseState isn't parent of inner, have to pass down
3345			 * the query text by hand.
3346			 */
3347			sub_pstate->p_sourcetext = queryString;
3348
3349			/*
3350			 * Set up OLD/NEW in the rtable for this statement.  The entries
3351			 * are added only to relnamespace, not varnamespace, because we
3352			 * don't want them to be referred to by unqualified field names
3353			 * nor "*" in the rule actions.  We decide later whether to put
3354			 * them in the joinlist.
3355			 */
3356			oldnsitem = addRangeTableEntryForRelation(sub_pstate, rel,
3357													  AccessShareLock,
3358													  makeAlias("old", NIL),
3359													  false, false);
3360			newnsitem = addRangeTableEntryForRelation(sub_pstate, rel,
3361													  AccessShareLock,
3362													  makeAlias("new", NIL),
3363													  false, false);
3364			addNSItemToQuery(sub_pstate, oldnsitem, false, true, false);
3365			addNSItemToQuery(sub_pstate, newnsitem, false, true, false);
3366
3367			/* Transform the rule action statement */
3368			top_subqry = transformStmt(sub_pstate, action);
3369
3370			/*
3371			 * We cannot support utility-statement actions (eg NOTIFY) with
3372			 * nonempty rule WHERE conditions, because there's no way to make
3373			 * the utility action execute conditionally.
3374			 */
3375			if (top_subqry->commandType == CMD_UTILITY &&
3376				*whereClause != NULL)
3377				ereport(ERROR,
3378						(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
3379						 errmsg("rules with WHERE conditions can only have SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE actions")));
3380
3381			/*
3382			 * If the action is INSERT...SELECT, OLD/NEW have been pushed down
3383			 * into the SELECT, and that's what we need to look at. (Ugly
3384			 * kluge ... try to fix this when we redesign querytrees.)
3385			 */
3386			sub_qry = getInsertSelectQuery(top_subqry, NULL);
3387
3388			/*
3389			 * If the sub_qry is a setop, we cannot attach any qualifications
3390			 * to it, because the planner won't notice them.  This could
3391			 * perhaps be relaxed someday, but for now, we may as well reject
3392			 * such a rule immediately.
3393			 */
3394			if (sub_qry->setOperations != NULL && *whereClause != NULL)
3395				ereport(ERROR,
3396						(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3397						 errmsg("conditional UNION/INTERSECT/EXCEPT statements are not implemented")));
3398
3399			/*
3400			 * Validate action's use of OLD/NEW, qual too
3401			 */
3402			has_old =
3403				rangeTableEntry_used((Node *) sub_qry, PRS2_OLD_VARNO, 0) ||
3404				rangeTableEntry_used(*whereClause, PRS2_OLD_VARNO, 0);
3405			has_new =
3406				rangeTableEntry_used((Node *) sub_qry, PRS2_NEW_VARNO, 0) ||
3407				rangeTableEntry_used(*whereClause, PRS2_NEW_VARNO, 0);
3408
3409			switch (stmt->event)
3410			{
3411				case CMD_SELECT:
3412					if (has_old)
3413						ereport(ERROR,
3414								(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
3415								 errmsg("ON SELECT rule cannot use OLD")));
3416					if (has_new)
3417						ereport(ERROR,
3418								(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
3419								 errmsg("ON SELECT rule cannot use NEW")));
3420					break;
3421				case CMD_UPDATE:
3422					/* both are OK */
3423					break;
3424				case CMD_INSERT:
3425					if (has_old)
3426						ereport(ERROR,
3427								(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
3428								 errmsg("ON INSERT rule cannot use OLD")));
3429					break;
3430				case CMD_DELETE:
3431					if (has_new)
3432						ereport(ERROR,
3433								(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
3434								 errmsg("ON DELETE rule cannot use NEW")));
3435					break;
3436				default:
3437					elog(ERROR, "unrecognized event type: %d",
3438						 (int) stmt->event);
3439					break;
3440			}
3441
3442			/*
3443			 * OLD/NEW are not allowed in WITH queries, because they would
3444			 * amount to outer references for the WITH, which we disallow.
3445			 * However, they were already in the outer rangetable when we
3446			 * analyzed the query, so we have to check.
3447			 *
3448			 * Note that in the INSERT...SELECT case, we need to examine the
3449			 * CTE lists of both top_subqry and sub_qry.
3450			 *
3451			 * Note that we aren't digging into the body of the query looking
3452			 * for WITHs in nested sub-SELECTs.  A WITH down there can
3453			 * legitimately refer to OLD/NEW, because it'd be an
3454			 * indirect-correlated outer reference.
3455			 */
3456			if (rangeTableEntry_used((Node *) top_subqry->cteList,
3457									 PRS2_OLD_VARNO, 0) ||
3458				rangeTableEntry_used((Node *) sub_qry->cteList,
3459									 PRS2_OLD_VARNO, 0))
3460				ereport(ERROR,
3461						(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3462						 errmsg("cannot refer to OLD within WITH query")));
3463			if (rangeTableEntry_used((Node *) top_subqry->cteList,
3464									 PRS2_NEW_VARNO, 0) ||
3465				rangeTableEntry_used((Node *) sub_qry->cteList,
3466									 PRS2_NEW_VARNO, 0))
3467				ereport(ERROR,
3468						(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3469						 errmsg("cannot refer to NEW within WITH query")));
3470
3471			/*
3472			 * For efficiency's sake, add OLD to the rule action's jointree
3473			 * only if it was actually referenced in the statement or qual.
3474			 *
3475			 * For INSERT, NEW is not really a relation (only a reference to
3476			 * the to-be-inserted tuple) and should never be added to the
3477			 * jointree.
3478			 *
3479			 * For UPDATE, we treat NEW as being another kind of reference to
3480			 * OLD, because it represents references to *transformed* tuples
3481			 * of the existing relation.  It would be wrong to enter NEW
3482			 * separately in the jointree, since that would cause a double
3483			 * join of the updated relation.  It's also wrong to fail to make
3484			 * a jointree entry if only NEW and not OLD is mentioned.
3485			 */
3486			if (has_old || (has_new && stmt->event == CMD_UPDATE))
3487			{
3488				RangeTblRef *rtr;
3489
3490				/*
3491				 * If sub_qry is a setop, manipulating its jointree will do no
3492				 * good at all, because the jointree is dummy. (This should be
3493				 * a can't-happen case because of prior tests.)
3494				 */
3495				if (sub_qry->setOperations != NULL)
3496					ereport(ERROR,
3497							(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
3498							 errmsg("conditional UNION/INTERSECT/EXCEPT statements are not implemented")));
3499				/* hackishly add OLD to the already-built FROM clause */
3500				rtr = makeNode(RangeTblRef);
3501				rtr->rtindex = oldnsitem->p_rtindex;
3502				sub_qry->jointree->fromlist =
3503					lappend(sub_qry->jointree->fromlist, rtr);
3504			}
3505
3506			newactions = lappend(newactions, top_subqry);
3507
3508			free_parsestate(sub_pstate);
3509		}
3510
3511		*actions = newactions;
3512	}
3513
3514	free_parsestate(pstate);
3515
3516	/* Close relation, but keep the exclusive lock */
3517	table_close(rel, NoLock);
3518}
3519
3520
3521/*
3522 * transformAlterTableStmt -
3523 *		parse analysis for ALTER TABLE
3524 *
3525 * Returns the transformed AlterTableStmt.  There may be additional actions
3526 * to be done before and after the transformed statement, which are returned
3527 * in *beforeStmts and *afterStmts as lists of utility command parsetrees.
3528 *
3529 * To avoid race conditions, it's important that this function rely only on
3530 * the passed-in relid (and not on stmt->relation) to determine the target
3531 * relation.
3532 */
3533AlterTableStmt *
3534transformAlterTableStmt(Oid relid, AlterTableStmt *stmt,
3535						const char *queryString,
3536						List **beforeStmts, List **afterStmts)
3537{
3538	Relation	rel;
3539	TupleDesc	tupdesc;
3540	ParseState *pstate;
3541	CreateStmtContext cxt;
3542	List	   *save_alist;
3543	ListCell   *lcmd,
3544			   *l;
3545	List	   *newcmds = NIL;
3546	bool		skipValidation = true;
3547	AlterTableCmd *newcmd;
3548	ParseNamespaceItem *nsitem;
3549
3550	/* Caller is responsible for locking the relation */
3551	rel = relation_open(relid, NoLock);
3552	tupdesc = RelationGetDescr(rel);
3553
3554	/* Set up pstate */
3555	pstate = make_parsestate(NULL);
3556	pstate->p_sourcetext = queryString;
3557	nsitem = addRangeTableEntryForRelation(pstate,
3558										   rel,
3559										   AccessShareLock,
3560										   NULL,
3561										   false,
3562										   true);
3563	addNSItemToQuery(pstate, nsitem, false, true, true);
3564
3565	/* Set up CreateStmtContext */
3566	cxt.pstate = pstate;
3567	if (rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_FOREIGN_TABLE)
3568	{
3569		cxt.stmtType = "ALTER FOREIGN TABLE";
3570		cxt.isforeign = true;
3571	}
3572	else
3573	{
3574		cxt.stmtType = "ALTER TABLE";
3575		cxt.isforeign = false;
3576	}
3577	cxt.relation = stmt->relation;
3578	cxt.rel = rel;
3579	cxt.inhRelations = NIL;
3580	cxt.isalter = true;
3581	cxt.columns = NIL;
3582	cxt.ckconstraints = NIL;
3583	cxt.nnconstraints = NIL;
3584	cxt.fkconstraints = NIL;
3585	cxt.ixconstraints = NIL;
3586	cxt.likeclauses = NIL;
3587	cxt.blist = NIL;
3588	cxt.alist = NIL;
3589	cxt.pkey = NULL;
3590	cxt.ispartitioned = (rel->rd_rel->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE);
3591	cxt.partbound = NULL;
3592	cxt.ofType = false;
3593
3594	/*
3595	 * Transform ALTER subcommands that need it (most don't).  These largely
3596	 * re-use code from CREATE TABLE.
3597	 */
3598	foreach(lcmd, stmt->cmds)
3599	{
3600		AlterTableCmd *cmd = (AlterTableCmd *) lfirst(lcmd);
3601
3602		switch (cmd->subtype)
3603		{
3604			case AT_AddColumn:
3605				{
3606					ColumnDef  *def = castNode(ColumnDef, cmd->def);
3607
3608					transformColumnDefinition(&cxt, def);
3609
3610					/*
3611					 * If the column has a non-null default, we can't skip
3612					 * validation of foreign keys.
3613					 */
3614					if (def->raw_default != NULL)
3615						skipValidation = false;
3616
3617					/*
3618					 * All constraints are processed in other ways. Remove the
3619					 * original list
3620					 */
3621					def->constraints = NIL;
3622
3623					newcmds = lappend(newcmds, cmd);
3624					break;
3625				}
3626
3627			case AT_AddConstraint:
3628
3629				/*
3630				 * The original AddConstraint cmd node doesn't go to newcmds
3631				 */
3632				if (IsA(cmd->def, Constraint))
3633				{
3634					transformTableConstraint(&cxt, (Constraint *) cmd->def);
3635					if (((Constraint *) cmd->def)->contype == CONSTR_FOREIGN)
3636						skipValidation = false;
3637				}
3638				else
3639					elog(ERROR, "unrecognized node type: %d",
3640						 (int) nodeTag(cmd->def));
3641				break;
3642
3643			case AT_AlterColumnType:
3644				{
3645					ColumnDef  *def = castNode(ColumnDef, cmd->def);
3646					AttrNumber	attnum;
3647
3648					/*
3649					 * For ALTER COLUMN TYPE, transform the USING clause if
3650					 * one was specified.
3651					 */
3652					if (def->raw_default)
3653					{
3654						def->cooked_default =
3655							transformExpr(pstate, def->raw_default,
3656										  EXPR_KIND_ALTER_COL_TRANSFORM);
3657					}
3658
3659					/*
3660					 * For identity column, create ALTER SEQUENCE command to
3661					 * change the data type of the sequence. Identity sequence
3662					 * is associated with the top level partitioned table.
3663					 * Hence ignore partitions.
3664					 */
3665					if (!RelationGetForm(rel)->relispartition)
3666					{
3667						attnum = get_attnum(relid, cmd->name);
3668						if (attnum == InvalidAttrNumber)
3669							ereport(ERROR,
3670									(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_COLUMN),
3671									 errmsg("column \"%s\" of relation \"%s\" does not exist",
3672											cmd->name, RelationGetRelationName(rel))));
3673
3674						if (attnum > 0 &&
3675							TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, attnum - 1)->attidentity)
3676						{
3677							Oid			seq_relid = getIdentitySequence(rel, attnum, false);
3678							Oid			typeOid = typenameTypeId(pstate, def->typeName);
3679							AlterSeqStmt *altseqstmt = makeNode(AlterSeqStmt);
3680
3681							altseqstmt->sequence
3682								= makeRangeVar(get_namespace_name(get_rel_namespace(seq_relid)),
3683											   get_rel_name(seq_relid),
3684											   -1);
3685							altseqstmt->options = list_make1(makeDefElem("as",
3686																		 (Node *) makeTypeNameFromOid(typeOid, -1),
3687																		 -1));
3688							altseqstmt->for_identity = true;
3689							cxt.blist = lappend(cxt.blist, altseqstmt);
3690						}
3691					}
3692
3693					newcmds = lappend(newcmds, cmd);
3694					break;
3695				}
3696
3697			case AT_AddIdentity:
3698				{
3699					Constraint *def = castNode(Constraint, cmd->def);
3700					ColumnDef  *newdef = makeNode(ColumnDef);
3701					AttrNumber	attnum;
3702
3703					newdef->colname = cmd->name;
3704					newdef->identity = def->generated_when;
3705					cmd->def = (Node *) newdef;
3706
3707					attnum = get_attnum(relid, cmd->name);
3708					if (attnum == InvalidAttrNumber)
3709						ereport(ERROR,
3710								(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_COLUMN),
3711								 errmsg("column \"%s\" of relation \"%s\" does not exist",
3712										cmd->name, RelationGetRelationName(rel))));
3713
3714					generateSerialExtraStmts(&cxt, newdef,
3715											 get_atttype(relid, attnum),
3716											 def->options, true, true,
3717											 NULL, NULL);
3718
3719					newcmds = lappend(newcmds, cmd);
3720					break;
3721				}
3722
3723			case AT_SetIdentity:
3724				{
3725					/*
3726					 * Create an ALTER SEQUENCE statement for the internal
3727					 * sequence of the identity column.
3728					 */
3729					ListCell   *lc;
3730					List	   *newseqopts = NIL;
3731					List	   *newdef = NIL;
3732					AttrNumber	attnum;
3733					Oid			seq_relid;
3734
3735					/*
3736					 * Split options into those handled by ALTER SEQUENCE and
3737					 * those for ALTER TABLE proper.
3738					 */
3739					foreach(lc, castNode(List, cmd->def))
3740					{
3741						DefElem    *def = lfirst_node(DefElem, lc);
3742
3743						if (strcmp(def->defname, "generated") == 0)
3744							newdef = lappend(newdef, def);
3745						else
3746							newseqopts = lappend(newseqopts, def);
3747					}
3748
3749					attnum = get_attnum(relid, cmd->name);
3750					if (attnum == InvalidAttrNumber)
3751						ereport(ERROR,
3752								(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_COLUMN),
3753								 errmsg("column \"%s\" of relation \"%s\" does not exist",
3754										cmd->name, RelationGetRelationName(rel))));
3755
3756					seq_relid = getIdentitySequence(rel, attnum, true);
3757
3758					if (seq_relid)
3759					{
3760						AlterSeqStmt *seqstmt;
3761
3762						seqstmt = makeNode(AlterSeqStmt);
3763						seqstmt->sequence = makeRangeVar(get_namespace_name(get_rel_namespace(seq_relid)),
3764														 get_rel_name(seq_relid), -1);
3765						seqstmt->options = newseqopts;
3766						seqstmt->for_identity = true;
3767						seqstmt->missing_ok = false;
3768
3769						cxt.blist = lappend(cxt.blist, seqstmt);
3770					}
3771
3772					/*
3773					 * If column was not an identity column, we just let the
3774					 * ALTER TABLE command error out later.  (There are cases
3775					 * this fails to cover, but we'll need to restructure
3776					 * where creation of the sequence dependency linkage
3777					 * happens before we can fix it.)
3778					 */
3779
3780					cmd->def = (Node *) newdef;
3781					newcmds = lappend(newcmds, cmd);
3782					break;
3783				}
3784
3785			case AT_AttachPartition:
3786			case AT_DetachPartition:
3787				{
3788					PartitionCmd *partcmd = (PartitionCmd *) cmd->def;
3789
3790					transformPartitionCmd(&cxt, partcmd);
3791					/* assign transformed value of the partition bound */
3792					partcmd->bound = cxt.partbound;
3793				}
3794
3795				newcmds = lappend(newcmds, cmd);
3796				break;
3797
3798			default:
3799
3800				/*
3801				 * Currently, we shouldn't actually get here for subcommand
3802				 * types that don't require transformation; but if we do, just
3803				 * emit them unchanged.
3804				 */
3805				newcmds = lappend(newcmds, cmd);
3806				break;
3807		}
3808	}
3809
3810	/*
3811	 * Transfer anything we already have in cxt.alist into save_alist, to keep
3812	 * it separate from the output of transformIndexConstraints.
3813	 */
3814	save_alist = cxt.alist;
3815	cxt.alist = NIL;
3816
3817	/* Postprocess constraints */
3818	transformIndexConstraints(&cxt);
3819	transformFKConstraints(&cxt, skipValidation, true);
3820	transformCheckConstraints(&cxt, false);
3821
3822	/*
3823	 * Push any index-creation commands into the ALTER, so that they can be
3824	 * scheduled nicely by tablecmds.c.  Note that tablecmds.c assumes that
3825	 * the IndexStmt attached to an AT_AddIndex or AT_AddIndexConstraint
3826	 * subcommand has already been through transformIndexStmt.
3827	 */
3828	foreach(l, cxt.alist)
3829	{
3830		Node	   *istmt = (Node *) lfirst(l);
3831
3832		/*
3833		 * We assume here that cxt.alist contains only IndexStmts generated
3834		 * from primary key constraints.
3835		 */
3836		if (IsA(istmt, IndexStmt))
3837		{
3838			IndexStmt  *idxstmt = (IndexStmt *) istmt;
3839
3840			idxstmt = transformIndexStmt(relid, idxstmt, queryString);
3841			newcmd = makeNode(AlterTableCmd);
3842			newcmd->subtype = OidIsValid(idxstmt->indexOid) ? AT_AddIndexConstraint : AT_AddIndex;
3843			newcmd->def = (Node *) idxstmt;
3844			newcmds = lappend(newcmds, newcmd);
3845		}
3846		else
3847			elog(ERROR, "unexpected stmt type %d", (int) nodeTag(istmt));
3848	}
3849	cxt.alist = NIL;
3850
3851	/* Append any CHECK, NOT NULL or FK constraints to the commands list */
3852	foreach_node(Constraint, def, cxt.ckconstraints)
3853	{
3854		newcmd = makeNode(AlterTableCmd);
3855		newcmd->subtype = AT_AddConstraint;
3856		newcmd->def = (Node *) def;
3857		newcmds = lappend(newcmds, newcmd);
3858	}
3859	foreach_node(Constraint, def, cxt.nnconstraints)
3860	{
3861		newcmd = makeNode(AlterTableCmd);
3862		newcmd->subtype = AT_AddConstraint;
3863		newcmd->def = (Node *) def;
3864		newcmds = lappend(newcmds, newcmd);
3865	}
3866	foreach_node(Constraint, def, cxt.fkconstraints)
3867	{
3868		newcmd = makeNode(AlterTableCmd);
3869		newcmd->subtype = AT_AddConstraint;
3870		newcmd->def = (Node *) def;
3871		newcmds = lappend(newcmds, newcmd);
3872	}
3873
3874	/* Close rel */
3875	relation_close(rel, NoLock);
3876
3877	/*
3878	 * Output results.
3879	 */
3880	stmt->cmds = newcmds;
3881
3882	*beforeStmts = cxt.blist;
3883	*afterStmts = list_concat(cxt.alist, save_alist);
3884
3885	return stmt;
3886}
3887
3888
3889/*
3890 * Preprocess a list of column constraint clauses
3891 * to attach constraint attributes to their primary constraint nodes
3892 * and detect inconsistent/misplaced constraint attributes.
3893 *
3894 * NOTE: currently, attributes are only supported for FOREIGN KEY, UNIQUE,
3895 * EXCLUSION, and PRIMARY KEY constraints, but someday they ought to be
3896 * supported for other constraint types.
3897 */
3898static void
3899transformConstraintAttrs(CreateStmtContext *cxt, List *constraintList)
3900{
3901	Constraint *lastprimarycon = NULL;
3902	bool		saw_deferrability = false;
3903	bool		saw_initially = false;
3904	bool		saw_enforced = false;
3905	ListCell   *clist;
3906
3907#define SUPPORTS_ATTRS(node)				\
3908	((node) != NULL &&						\
3909	 ((node)->contype == CONSTR_PRIMARY ||	\
3910	  (node)->contype == CONSTR_UNIQUE ||	\
3911	  (node)->contype == CONSTR_EXCLUSION || \
3912	  (node)->contype == CONSTR_FOREIGN))
3913
3914	foreach(clist, constraintList)
3915	{
3916		Constraint *con = (Constraint *) lfirst(clist);
3917
3918		if (!IsA(con, Constraint))
3919			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized node type: %d",
3920				 (int) nodeTag(con));
3921		switch (con->contype)
3922		{
3923			case CONSTR_ATTR_DEFERRABLE:
3924				if (!SUPPORTS_ATTRS(lastprimarycon))
3925					ereport(ERROR,
3926							(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
3927							 errmsg("misplaced DEFERRABLE clause"),
3928							 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, con->location)));
3929				if (saw_deferrability)
3930					ereport(ERROR,
3931							(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
3932							 errmsg("multiple DEFERRABLE/NOT DEFERRABLE clauses not allowed"),
3933							 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, con->location)));
3934				saw_deferrability = true;
3935				lastprimarycon->deferrable = true;
3936				break;
3937
3938			case CONSTR_ATTR_NOT_DEFERRABLE:
3939				if (!SUPPORTS_ATTRS(lastprimarycon))
3940					ereport(ERROR,
3941							(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
3942							 errmsg("misplaced NOT DEFERRABLE clause"),
3943							 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, con->location)));
3944				if (saw_deferrability)
3945					ereport(ERROR,
3946							(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
3947							 errmsg("multiple DEFERRABLE/NOT DEFERRABLE clauses not allowed"),
3948							 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, con->location)));
3949				saw_deferrability = true;
3950				lastprimarycon->deferrable = false;
3951				if (saw_initially &&
3952					lastprimarycon->initdeferred)
3953					ereport(ERROR,
3954							(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
3955							 errmsg("constraint declared INITIALLY DEFERRED must be DEFERRABLE"),
3956							 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, con->location)));
3957				break;
3958
3959			case CONSTR_ATTR_DEFERRED:
3960				if (!SUPPORTS_ATTRS(lastprimarycon))
3961					ereport(ERROR,
3962							(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
3963							 errmsg("misplaced INITIALLY DEFERRED clause"),
3964							 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, con->location)));
3965				if (saw_initially)
3966					ereport(ERROR,
3967							(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
3968							 errmsg("multiple INITIALLY IMMEDIATE/DEFERRED clauses not allowed"),
3969							 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, con->location)));
3970				saw_initially = true;
3971				lastprimarycon->initdeferred = true;
3972
3973				/*
3974				 * If only INITIALLY DEFERRED appears, assume DEFERRABLE
3975				 */
3976				if (!saw_deferrability)
3977					lastprimarycon->deferrable = true;
3978				else if (!lastprimarycon->deferrable)
3979					ereport(ERROR,
3980							(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
3981							 errmsg("constraint declared INITIALLY DEFERRED must be DEFERRABLE"),
3982							 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, con->location)));
3983				break;
3984
3985			case CONSTR_ATTR_IMMEDIATE:
3986				if (!SUPPORTS_ATTRS(lastprimarycon))
3987					ereport(ERROR,
3988							(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
3989							 errmsg("misplaced INITIALLY IMMEDIATE clause"),
3990							 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, con->location)));
3991				if (saw_initially)
3992					ereport(ERROR,
3993							(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
3994							 errmsg("multiple INITIALLY IMMEDIATE/DEFERRED clauses not allowed"),
3995							 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, con->location)));
3996				saw_initially = true;
3997				lastprimarycon->initdeferred = false;
3998				break;
3999
4000			case CONSTR_ATTR_ENFORCED:
4001				if (lastprimarycon == NULL ||
4002					(lastprimarycon->contype != CONSTR_CHECK &&
4003					 lastprimarycon->contype != CONSTR_FOREIGN))
4004					ereport(ERROR,
4005							(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
4006							 errmsg("misplaced ENFORCED clause"),
4007							 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, con->location)));
4008				if (saw_enforced)
4009					ereport(ERROR,
4010							(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
4011							 errmsg("multiple ENFORCED/NOT ENFORCED clauses not allowed"),
4012							 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, con->location)));
4013				saw_enforced = true;
4014				lastprimarycon->is_enforced = true;
4015				break;
4016
4017			case CONSTR_ATTR_NOT_ENFORCED:
4018				if (lastprimarycon == NULL ||
4019					(lastprimarycon->contype != CONSTR_CHECK &&
4020					 lastprimarycon->contype != CONSTR_FOREIGN))
4021					ereport(ERROR,
4022							(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
4023							 errmsg("misplaced NOT ENFORCED clause"),
4024							 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, con->location)));
4025				if (saw_enforced)
4026					ereport(ERROR,
4027							(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
4028							 errmsg("multiple ENFORCED/NOT ENFORCED clauses not allowed"),
4029							 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate, con->location)));
4030				saw_enforced = true;
4031				lastprimarycon->is_enforced = false;
4032
4033				/* A NOT ENFORCED constraint must be marked as invalid. */
4034				lastprimarycon->skip_validation = true;
4035				lastprimarycon->initially_valid = false;
4036				break;
4037
4038			default:
4039				/* Otherwise it's not an attribute */
4040				lastprimarycon = con;
4041				/* reset flags for new primary node */
4042				saw_deferrability = false;
4043				saw_initially = false;
4044				saw_enforced = false;
4045				break;
4046		}
4047	}
4048}
4049
4050/*
4051 * Special handling of type definition for a column
4052 */
4053static void
4054transformColumnType(CreateStmtContext *cxt, ColumnDef *column)
4055{
4056	/*
4057	 * All we really need to do here is verify that the type is valid,
4058	 * including any collation spec that might be present.
4059	 */
4060	Type		ctype = typenameType(cxt->pstate, column->typeName, NULL);
4061
4062	if (column->collClause)
4063	{
4064		Form_pg_type typtup = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(ctype);
4065
4066		LookupCollation(cxt->pstate,
4067						column->collClause->collname,
4068						column->collClause->location);
4069		/* Complain if COLLATE is applied to an uncollatable type */
4070		if (!OidIsValid(typtup->typcollation))
4071			ereport(ERROR,
4072					(errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
4073					 errmsg("collations are not supported by type %s",
4074							format_type_be(typtup->oid)),
4075					 parser_errposition(cxt->pstate,
4076										column->collClause->location)));
4077	}
4078
4079	ReleaseSysCache(ctype);
4080}
4081
4082
4083/*
4084 * transformCreateSchemaStmtElements -
4085 *	  analyzes the elements of a CREATE SCHEMA statement
4086 *
4087 * Split the schema element list from a CREATE SCHEMA statement into
4088 * individual commands and place them in the result list in an order
4089 * such that there are no forward references (e.g. GRANT to a table
4090 * created later in the list). Note that the logic we use for determining
4091 * forward references is presently quite incomplete.
4092 *
4093 * "schemaName" is the name of the schema that will be used for the creation
4094 * of the objects listed, that may be compiled from the schema name defined
4095 * in the statement or a role specification.
4096 *
4097 * SQL also allows constraints to make forward references, so thumb through
4098 * the table columns and move forward references to a posterior alter-table
4099 * command.
4100 *
4101 * The result is a list of parse nodes that still need to be analyzed ---
4102 * but we can't analyze the later commands until we've executed the earlier
4103 * ones, because of possible inter-object references.
4104 *
4105 * Note: this breaks the rules a little bit by modifying schema-name fields
4106 * within passed-in structs.  However, the transformation would be the same
4107 * if done over, so it should be all right to scribble on the input to this
4108 * extent.
4109 */
4110List *
4111transformCreateSchemaStmtElements(List *schemaElts, const char *schemaName)
4112{
4113	CreateSchemaStmtContext cxt;
4114	List	   *result;
4115	ListCell   *elements;
4116
4117	cxt.schemaname = schemaName;
4118	cxt.sequences = NIL;
4119	cxt.tables = NIL;
4120	cxt.views = NIL;
4121	cxt.indexes = NIL;
4122	cxt.triggers = NIL;
4123	cxt.grants = NIL;
4124
4125	/*
4126	 * Run through each schema element in the schema element list. Separate
4127	 * statements by type, and do preliminary analysis.
4128	 */
4129	foreach(elements, schemaElts)
4130	{
4131		Node	   *element = lfirst(elements);
4132
4133		switch (nodeTag(element))
4134		{
4135			case T_CreateSeqStmt:
4136				{
4137					CreateSeqStmt *elp = (CreateSeqStmt *) element;
4138
4139					setSchemaName(cxt.schemaname, &elp->sequence->schemaname);
4140					cxt.sequences = lappend(cxt.sequences, element);
4141				}
4142				break;
4143
4144			case T_CreateStmt:
4145				{
4146					CreateStmt *elp = (CreateStmt *) element;
4147
4148					setSchemaName(cxt.schemaname, &elp->relation->schemaname);
4149
4150					/*
4151					 * XXX todo: deal with constraints
4152					 */
4153					cxt.tables = lappend(cxt.tables, element);
4154				}
4155				break;
4156
4157			case T_ViewStmt:
4158				{
4159					ViewStmt   *elp = (ViewStmt *) element;
4160
4161					setSchemaName(cxt.schemaname, &elp->view->schemaname);
4162
4163					/*
4164					 * XXX todo: deal with references between views
4165					 */
4166					cxt.views = lappend(cxt.views, element);
4167				}
4168				break;
4169
4170			case T_IndexStmt:
4171				{
4172					IndexStmt  *elp = (IndexStmt *) element;
4173
4174					setSchemaName(cxt.schemaname, &elp->relation->schemaname);
4175					cxt.indexes = lappend(cxt.indexes, element);
4176				}
4177				break;
4178
4179			case T_CreateTrigStmt:
4180				{
4181					CreateTrigStmt *elp = (CreateTrigStmt *) element;
4182
4183					setSchemaName(cxt.schemaname, &elp->relation->schemaname);
4184					cxt.triggers = lappend(cxt.triggers, element);
4185				}
4186				break;
4187
4188			case T_GrantStmt:
4189				cxt.grants = lappend(cxt.grants, element);
4190				break;
4191
4192			default:
4193				elog(ERROR, "unrecognized node type: %d",
4194					 (int) nodeTag(element));
4195		}
4196	}
4197
4198	result = NIL;
4199	result = list_concat(result, cxt.sequences);
4200	result = list_concat(result, cxt.tables);
4201	result = list_concat(result, cxt.views);
4202	result = list_concat(result, cxt.indexes);
4203	result = list_concat(result, cxt.triggers);
4204	result = list_concat(result, cxt.grants);
4205
4206	return result;
4207}
4208
4209/*
4210 * setSchemaName
4211 *		Set or check schema name in an element of a CREATE SCHEMA command
4212 */
4213static void
4214setSchemaName(const char *context_schema, char **stmt_schema_name)
4215{
4216	if (*stmt_schema_name == NULL)
4217		*stmt_schema_name = unconstify(char *, context_schema);
4218	else if (strcmp(context_schema, *stmt_schema_name) != 0)
4219		ereport(ERROR,
4220				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_SCHEMA_DEFINITION),
4221				 errmsg("CREATE specifies a schema (%s) "
4222						"different from the one being created (%s)",
4223						*stmt_schema_name, context_schema)));
4224}
4225
4226/*
4227 * transformPartitionCmd
4228 *		Analyze the ATTACH/DETACH PARTITION command
4229 *
4230 * In case of the ATTACH PARTITION command, cxt->partbound is set to the
4231 * transformed value of cmd->bound.
4232 */
4233static void
4234transformPartitionCmd(CreateStmtContext *cxt, PartitionCmd *cmd)
4235{
4236	Relation	parentRel = cxt->rel;
4237
4238	switch (parentRel->rd_rel->relkind)
4239	{
4240		case RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE:
4241			/* transform the partition bound, if any */
4242			Assert(RelationGetPartitionKey(parentRel) != NULL);
4243			if (cmd->bound != NULL)
4244				cxt->partbound = transformPartitionBound(cxt->pstate, parentRel,
4245														 cmd->bound);
4246			break;
4247		case RELKIND_PARTITIONED_INDEX:
4248
4249			/*
4250			 * A partitioned index cannot have a partition bound set.  ALTER
4251			 * INDEX prevents that with its grammar, but not ALTER TABLE.
4252			 */
4253			if (cmd->bound != NULL)
4254				ereport(ERROR,
4255						(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
4256						 errmsg("\"%s\" is not a partitioned table",
4257								RelationGetRelationName(parentRel))));
4258			break;
4259		case RELKIND_RELATION:
4260			/* the table must be partitioned */
4261			ereport(ERROR,
4262					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
4263					 errmsg("table \"%s\" is not partitioned",
4264							RelationGetRelationName(parentRel))));
4265			break;
4266		case RELKIND_INDEX:
4267			/* the index must be partitioned */
4268			ereport(ERROR,
4269					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
4270					 errmsg("index \"%s\" is not partitioned",
4271							RelationGetRelationName(parentRel))));
4272			break;
4273		default:
4274			/* parser shouldn't let this case through */
4275			elog(ERROR, "\"%s\" is not a partitioned table or index",
4276				 RelationGetRelationName(parentRel));
4277			break;
4278	}
4279}
4280
4281/*
4282 * transformPartitionBound
4283 *
4284 * Transform a partition bound specification
4285 */
4286PartitionBoundSpec *
4287transformPartitionBound(ParseState *pstate, Relation parent,
4288						PartitionBoundSpec *spec)
4289{
4290	PartitionBoundSpec *result_spec;
4291	PartitionKey key = RelationGetPartitionKey(parent);
4292	char		strategy = get_partition_strategy(key);
4293	int			partnatts = get_partition_natts(key);
4294	List	   *partexprs = get_partition_exprs(key);
4295
4296	/* Avoid scribbling on input */
4297	result_spec = copyObject(spec);
4298
4299	if (spec->is_default)
4300	{
4301		/*
4302		 * Hash partitioning does not support a default partition; there's no
4303		 * use case for it (since the set of partitions to create is perfectly
4304		 * defined), and if users do get into it accidentally, it's hard to
4305		 * back out from it afterwards.
4306		 */
4307		if (strategy == PARTITION_STRATEGY_HASH)
4308			ereport(ERROR,
4309					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_TABLE_DEFINITION),
4310					 errmsg("a hash-partitioned table may not have a default partition")));
4311
4312		/*
4313		 * In case of the default partition, parser had no way to identify the
4314		 * partition strategy. Assign the parent's strategy to the default
4315		 * partition bound spec.
4316		 */
4317		result_spec->strategy = strategy;
4318
4319		return result_spec;
4320	}
4321
4322	if (strategy == PARTITION_STRATEGY_HASH)
4323	{
4324		if (spec->strategy != PARTITION_STRATEGY_HASH)
4325			ereport(ERROR,
4326					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_TABLE_DEFINITION),
4327					 errmsg("invalid bound specification for a hash partition"),
4328					 parser_errposition(pstate, exprLocation((Node *) spec))));
4329
4330		if (spec->modulus <= 0)
4331			ereport(ERROR,
4332					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_TABLE_DEFINITION),
4333					 errmsg("modulus for hash partition must be an integer value greater than zero")));
4334
4335		Assert(spec->remainder >= 0);
4336
4337		if (spec->remainder >= spec->modulus)
4338			ereport(ERROR,
4339					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_TABLE_DEFINITION),
4340					 errmsg("remainder for hash partition must be less than modulus")));
4341	}
4342	else if (strategy == PARTITION_STRATEGY_LIST)
4343	{
4344		ListCell   *cell;
4345		char	   *colname;
4346		Oid			coltype;
4347		int32		coltypmod;
4348		Oid			partcollation;
4349
4350		if (spec->strategy != PARTITION_STRATEGY_LIST)
4351			ereport(ERROR,
4352					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_TABLE_DEFINITION),
4353					 errmsg("invalid bound specification for a list partition"),
4354					 parser_errposition(pstate, exprLocation((Node *) spec))));
4355
4356		/* Get the only column's name in case we need to output an error */
4357		if (key->partattrs[0] != 0)
4358			colname = get_attname(RelationGetRelid(parent),
4359								  key->partattrs[0], false);
4360		else
4361			colname = deparse_expression((Node *) linitial(partexprs),
4362										 deparse_context_for(RelationGetRelationName(parent),
4363															 RelationGetRelid(parent)),
4364										 false, false);
4365		/* Need its type data too */
4366		coltype = get_partition_col_typid(key, 0);
4367		coltypmod = get_partition_col_typmod(key, 0);
4368		partcollation = get_partition_col_collation(key, 0);
4369
4370		result_spec->listdatums = NIL;
4371		foreach(cell, spec->listdatums)
4372		{
4373			Node	   *expr = lfirst(cell);
4374			Const	   *value;
4375			ListCell   *cell2;
4376			bool		duplicate;
4377
4378			value = transformPartitionBoundValue(pstate, expr,
4379												 colname, coltype, coltypmod,
4380												 partcollation);
4381
4382			/* Don't add to the result if the value is a duplicate */
4383			duplicate = false;
4384			foreach(cell2, result_spec->listdatums)
4385			{
4386				Const	   *value2 = lfirst_node(Const, cell2);
4387
4388				if (equal(value, value2))
4389				{
4390					duplicate = true;
4391					break;
4392				}
4393			}
4394			if (duplicate)
4395				continue;
4396
4397			result_spec->listdatums = lappend(result_spec->listdatums,
4398											  value);
4399		}
4400	}
4401	else if (strategy == PARTITION_STRATEGY_RANGE)
4402	{
4403		if (spec->strategy != PARTITION_STRATEGY_RANGE)
4404			ereport(ERROR,
4405					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_TABLE_DEFINITION),
4406					 errmsg("invalid bound specification for a range partition"),
4407					 parser_errposition(pstate, exprLocation((Node *) spec))));
4408
4409		if (list_length(spec->lowerdatums) != partnatts)
4410			ereport(ERROR,
4411					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_TABLE_DEFINITION),
4412					 errmsg("FROM must specify exactly one value per partitioning column")));
4413		if (list_length(spec->upperdatums) != partnatts)
4414			ereport(ERROR,
4415					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_TABLE_DEFINITION),
4416					 errmsg("TO must specify exactly one value per partitioning column")));
4417
4418		/*
4419		 * Convert raw parse nodes into PartitionRangeDatum nodes and perform
4420		 * any necessary validation.
4421		 */
4422		result_spec->lowerdatums =
4423			transformPartitionRangeBounds(pstate, spec->lowerdatums,
4424										  parent);
4425		result_spec->upperdatums =
4426			transformPartitionRangeBounds(pstate, spec->upperdatums,
4427										  parent);
4428	}
4429	else
4430		elog(ERROR, "unexpected partition strategy: %d", (int) strategy);
4431
4432	return result_spec;
4433}
4434
4435/*
4436 * transformPartitionRangeBounds
4437 *		This converts the expressions for range partition bounds from the raw
4438 *		grammar representation to PartitionRangeDatum structs
4439 */
4440static List *
4441transformPartitionRangeBounds(ParseState *pstate, List *blist,
4442							  Relation parent)
4443{
4444	List	   *result = NIL;
4445	PartitionKey key = RelationGetPartitionKey(parent);
4446	List	   *partexprs = get_partition_exprs(key);
4447	ListCell   *lc;
4448	int			i,
4449				j;
4450
4451	j = 0;
4452	foreach(lc, blist)
4453	{
4454		Node	   *expr = lfirst(lc);
4455		PartitionRangeDatum *prd = NULL;
4456
4457		i = foreach_current_index(lc);
4458
4459		/*
4460		 * Infinite range bounds -- "minvalue" and "maxvalue" -- get passed in
4461		 * as ColumnRefs.
4462		 */
4463		if (IsA(expr, ColumnRef))
4464		{
4465			ColumnRef  *cref = (ColumnRef *) expr;
4466			char	   *cname = NULL;
4467
4468			/*
4469			 * There should be a single field named either "minvalue" or
4470			 * "maxvalue".
4471			 */
4472			if (list_length(cref->fields) == 1 &&
4473				IsA(linitial(cref->fields), String))
4474				cname = strVal(linitial(cref->fields));
4475
4476			if (cname == NULL)
4477			{
4478				/*
4479				 * ColumnRef is not in the desired single-field-name form. For
4480				 * consistency between all partition strategies, let the
4481				 * expression transformation report any errors rather than
4482				 * doing it ourselves.
4483				 */
4484			}
4485			else if (strcmp("minvalue", cname) == 0)
4486			{
4487				prd = makeNode(PartitionRangeDatum);
4488				prd->kind = PARTITION_RANGE_DATUM_MINVALUE;
4489				prd->value = NULL;
4490			}
4491			else if (strcmp("maxvalue", cname) == 0)
4492			{
4493				prd = makeNode(PartitionRangeDatum);
4494				prd->kind = PARTITION_RANGE_DATUM_MAXVALUE;
4495				prd->value = NULL;
4496			}
4497		}
4498
4499		if (prd == NULL)
4500		{
4501			char	   *colname;
4502			Oid			coltype;
4503			int32		coltypmod;
4504			Oid			partcollation;
4505			Const	   *value;
4506
4507			/* Get the column's name in case we need to output an error */
4508			if (key->partattrs[i] != 0)
4509				colname = get_attname(RelationGetRelid(parent),
4510									  key->partattrs[i], false);
4511			else
4512			{
4513				colname = deparse_expression((Node *) list_nth(partexprs, j),
4514											 deparse_context_for(RelationGetRelationName(parent),
4515																 RelationGetRelid(parent)),
4516											 false, false);
4517				++j;
4518			}
4519
4520			/* Need its type data too */
4521			coltype = get_partition_col_typid(key, i);
4522			coltypmod = get_partition_col_typmod(key, i);
4523			partcollation = get_partition_col_collation(key, i);
4524
4525			value = transformPartitionBoundValue(pstate, expr,
4526												 colname,
4527												 coltype, coltypmod,
4528												 partcollation);
4529			if (value->constisnull)
4530				ereport(ERROR,
4531						(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
4532						 errmsg("cannot specify NULL in range bound")));
4533			prd = makeNode(PartitionRangeDatum);
4534			prd->kind = PARTITION_RANGE_DATUM_VALUE;
4535			prd->value = (Node *) value;
4536		}
4537
4538		prd->location = exprLocation(expr);
4539
4540		result = lappend(result, prd);
4541	}
4542
4543	/*
4544	 * Once we see MINVALUE or MAXVALUE for one column, the remaining columns
4545	 * must be the same.
4546	 */
4547	validateInfiniteBounds(pstate, result);
4548
4549	return result;
4550}
4551
4552/*
4553 * validateInfiniteBounds
4554 *
4555 * Check that a MAXVALUE or MINVALUE specification in a partition bound is
4556 * followed only by more of the same.
4557 */
4558static void
4559validateInfiniteBounds(ParseState *pstate, List *blist)
4560{
4561	ListCell   *lc;
4562	PartitionRangeDatumKind kind = PARTITION_RANGE_DATUM_VALUE;
4563
4564	foreach(lc, blist)
4565	{
4566		PartitionRangeDatum *prd = lfirst_node(PartitionRangeDatum, lc);
4567
4568		if (kind == prd->kind)
4569			continue;
4570
4571		switch (kind)
4572		{
4573			case PARTITION_RANGE_DATUM_VALUE:
4574				kind = prd->kind;
4575				break;
4576
4577			case PARTITION_RANGE_DATUM_MAXVALUE:
4578				ereport(ERROR,
4579						(errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
4580						 errmsg("every bound following MAXVALUE must also be MAXVALUE"),
4581						 parser_errposition(pstate, exprLocation((Node *) prd))));
4582				break;
4583
4584			case PARTITION_RANGE_DATUM_MINVALUE:
4585				ereport(ERROR,
4586						(errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
4587						 errmsg("every bound following MINVALUE must also be MINVALUE"),
4588						 parser_errposition(pstate, exprLocation((Node *) prd))));
4589				break;
4590		}
4591	}
4592}
4593
4594/*
4595 * Transform one entry in a partition bound spec, producing a constant.
4596 */
4597static Const *
4598transformPartitionBoundValue(ParseState *pstate, Node *val,
4599							 const char *colName, Oid colType, int32 colTypmod,
4600							 Oid partCollation)
4601{
4602	Node	   *value;
4603
4604	/* Transform raw parsetree */
4605	value = transformExpr(pstate, val, EXPR_KIND_PARTITION_BOUND);
4606
4607	/*
4608	 * transformExpr() should have already rejected column references,
4609	 * subqueries, aggregates, window functions, and SRFs, based on the
4610	 * EXPR_KIND_ of a partition bound expression.
4611	 */
4612	Assert(!contain_var_clause(value));
4613
4614	/*
4615	 * Coerce to the correct type.  This might cause an explicit coercion step
4616	 * to be added on top of the expression, which must be evaluated before
4617	 * returning the result to the caller.
4618	 */
4619	value = coerce_to_target_type(pstate,
4620								  value, exprType(value),
4621								  colType,
4622								  colTypmod,
4623								  COERCION_ASSIGNMENT,
4624								  COERCE_IMPLICIT_CAST,
4625								  -1);
4626
4627	if (value == NULL)
4628		ereport(ERROR,
4629				(errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
4630				 errmsg("specified value cannot be cast to type %s for column \"%s\"",
4631						format_type_be(colType), colName),
4632				 parser_errposition(pstate, exprLocation(val))));
4633
4634	/*
4635	 * Evaluate the expression, if needed, assigning the partition key's data
4636	 * type and collation to the resulting Const node.
4637	 */
4638	if (!IsA(value, Const))
4639	{
4640		assign_expr_collations(pstate, value);
4641		value = (Node *) expression_planner((Expr *) value);
4642		value = (Node *) evaluate_expr((Expr *) value, colType, colTypmod,
4643									   partCollation);
4644		if (!IsA(value, Const))
4645			elog(ERROR, "could not evaluate partition bound expression");
4646	}
4647	else
4648	{
4649		/*
4650		 * If the expression is already a Const, as is often the case, we can
4651		 * skip the rather expensive steps above.  But we still have to insert
4652		 * the right collation, since coerce_to_target_type doesn't handle
4653		 * that.
4654		 */
4655		((Const *) value)->constcollid = partCollation;
4656	}
4657
4658	/*
4659	 * Attach original expression's parse location to the Const, so that
4660	 * that's what will be reported for any later errors related to this
4661	 * partition bound.
4662	 */
4663	((Const *) value)->location = exprLocation(val);
4664
4665	return (Const *) value;
4666}
